name: Pu'u Ualaka'a (Tantalus Sunset)
tagline: The best free sunset view over Honolulu — Waikiki, Diamond Head, and Pearl Harbor all at once.
bestTime: Sunset — arrive 30 min before for parking and a good spot
talkStory: Pu'u Ualaka'a means 'rolling sweet potato hill' — legend says farmers' sweet potatoes would roll down the steep slopes during harvest. The lookout sits at 1,048 feet on the remains of an ancient volcanic vent in the Tantalus crater complex. Round Top Drive, the winding road up, passes through a forest planted by the CCC in the 1930s that now feels like a tropical rainforest. Locals know this as the best free panoramic viewpoint on Oahu — Waikiki, Diamond Head, Pearl Harbor, and the Waianae Range all visible at once, especially stunning at sunset.
